An alternative to #56113 that does not include minor breaking changes to `gcd` or `lcm(::AbstractArray{<:Integer})` .","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56423/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56423/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2463071275","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/pull/56423#issuecomment-2463071275","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/56423","id":2463071275,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6Sz3wr","user":{"login":"JuergenWiemers","id":22220065,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyMjIwMDY1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22220065?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers","html_url":"https://github.com/JuergenWiemers","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/received_events","type":"User","user_view_type":"public","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-11-07T19:42:28Z","updated_at":"2024-11-07T19:42:28Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> GPT tells me both Mathematica and Maple also follow this convention lcm() == 1, but I do not have access to run either of these languages; maybe someone else can verify?

To add another data point, Maple accepts `lcm` with zero arguments and thinks the answer is
```Maple
> lcm();
                                                           1
```
Regarding Mathematica, I'm not sure if `LCM[Null]` is the right question to ask. Wouldn't that be similar to `lcm(nothing)` in Julia? I think `LCM[{}] == LCM[List[]]` (i.e., lcm of the empty list) might be semantically closer to `lcm(Integer[])`. It makes a bit of a difference: While `LCM[Null]` is returned unevaluated (which is Mathematica's way to say "I have no idea what to do with this, so here you have it back."), `LCM[List[])` returns the empty list:

```Mathematica
In[1]:= LCM[List[]] 
Out[1]:= {}
```

{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53371","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53371/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53371/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53371/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53371","id":2140393950,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5_k9He","number":53371,"title":"Immense precompilation slow down","user":{"login":"projekter","id":2815453,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjI4MTU0NTM=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2815453?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/projekter","html_url":"https://github.com/projekter","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/projekter/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/projekter/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/projekter/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/projekter/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/projekter/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/projekter/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/projekter/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/projekter/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/projekter/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":3,"created_at":"2024-02-17T18:05:55Z","updated_at":"2024-02-20T11:25:31Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"I just ran into an example where precompilation of my package suddenly took forever (usually ~5s, now I terminate it after a minute). I managed to boil it down to the following:

```julia
module precompilationhang

const XorM{X} = Union{<:X,Missing}

struct Type1{P,V<:AbstractVector{P},Vr<:XorM{V},Vc<:XorM{V}}
end

struct Type2{P,
    E1<:(AbstractVector{M} where M<:Type1{P}),
    E2<:(AbstractVector{M} where M<:Type1{P}),
    E3<:(AbstractVector{M} where M<:Type1{P}),
    E4<:(AbstractVector{M} where M<:Type1{P})}
    f1::Vector{E1}
    f2::Vector{E2}
    f3::Vector{Vector{E3}}
    f4::Vector{Vector{E4}}
end

end
```

Precompilation of this tiny package takes about 4:40 minutes.
Trying to simplify it further first looked as if it still has the same issue, but this was merely impatience. For example (all of the examples have just the one thing changed that I mention, the changes don't accumulate):
- `XorM = Union{X,Missing}` takes 13 seconds
- removing just the field `f1` takes 10 seconds
- removing `E1` and `f1` takes 5 seconds
- removing just the `Vc` type parameter from `Type1` takes 2 seconds

```
Julia Version 1.10.1
Commit 7790d6f064 (2024-02-13 20:41 UTC)
Build Info:
  Official https://julialang.org/ release
Platform Info:
  OS: Windows (x86_64-w64-mingw32)
  CPU: 16 × 11th Gen Intel(R) Core(TM) i7-11800H @ 2.30GHz
  WORD_SIZE: 64
  LIBM: libopenlibm
  LLVM: libLLVM-15.0.7 (ORCJIT, tigerlake)
Threads: 1 default, 0 interactive, 1 GC (on 16 virtual cores)
```","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53371/reactions","total_count":1,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":1},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53371/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1954010964","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53371#issuecomment-1954010964","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/53371","id":1954010964,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM50d9dU","user":{"login":"JuergenWiemers","id":22220065,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIyMjIwMDY1","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/22220065?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers","html_url":"https://github.com/JuergenWiemers","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/JuergenWiemers/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-02-20T11:25:31Z","updated_at":"2024-02-20T11:25:31Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"> Compared to what? 1.9, 1.10.0?

The example above runs instantaneously on 1.9.4 and takes several minutes on 1.10.0 (tested on Windows and Linux). The slowdown also occurs on master.

Bisected to c1d1bde5 (@N5N3).","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/1954010964/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}
